g.text: The tip flies much faster than the root!
Adjust the physics to see how lift changes.

span.style: background-color: #3b82f6; color: white; padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-weight: 800; font-size: 14px; margin-right: 15px; letter-spacing: 0.5px; text-transform: uppercase;

div.h3: The Rotor Blade as an Airfoil

div.p: A rotor blade is fundamentally just a long, thin airplane wing (an airfoil). As relative wind strikes the leading edge, the blade's angled Pitch (Angle of Attack) forces air downward. By Newton's Third Law, this downward push creates an equal and opposite upward force called Lift.
